rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenocyclization of olefinic amides with diselenides to give 3-(selenomethyl)iminoisobenzofurans via a radical cascade, constructing C-Se and C-O bonds in one step
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) to a radical cation that fragments into a PhSe• radical and PhSe+ cation; the alkyl radical formed after cyclization is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2 (and PhSe+ is reduced back to PhSe- at cathode).

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered alkyl radical D after SePh addition to alkene
benzylic/tertiary carbocation E after radical cyclization and anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh (Eox ≈ 2.0 V, lower than substrate 1a at 2.2 V) to a radical cation that splits into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds across the C=C of the olefinic amide to give an alkyl radical, which cyclizes onto the amide oxygen; the resulting radical is oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that deprotonates to product, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode and PhSe+ is reduced back to PhSe- for turnover.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: With 2.0 equiv TEMPO, no selenocyclization product (3a) was obtained — only trace; indicates radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: DPE-SePh adduct detected by GC-MS; 3a still formed in 45% yield, supporting a PhSe• radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of PhSeSePh (2a) at 2.0 V vs 1a at 2.2 V, indicating diselenide is preferentially oxidized at the anode

notes: Authors favor anodic oxidation of the diselenide (not the alkene) based on CV. An alternative would be direct oxidation of the olefinic amide to a radical cation followed by PhSe- attack, but CV data argue against this. Acid hydrolysis of imine product 3a gives the isobenzofuranone 4a; ester and 2-vinylbenzyl alcohol substrates also undergo analogous selenocyclization to give furan/lactone products.
